{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == 概要 == [[File:Sakatekunari-jinja 02.jpg|left|160px|参道]] [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[玉城町]]上田辺（かみたぬい）[[小字|字]]大山田二144-1辰巳出版（2013）：48ページ、牛尾崎池西隣の丘の上に鎮座する伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：60ページ。玉城町にある13の内宮摂末社のうちの1社である。内宮の摂社27社のうち第25位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。地域では「さかだいさん」と呼ばれ、親しまれている。 [[社殿]]は緩やかな[[石段]]の[[参道]]を登ったところにある。社地の面積は7反5畝12歩（≒7,478[[平方メートル|m2]]）、社殿は[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]である式内社研究会 編（1990）：280ページ。[[玉垣]]と[[鳥居]]を備える。古代の社地は5町四方（≒49,585m2）、社殿は長さ5尺（≒1.51m）×広さ3.6尺（≒1.09m）×高さ4.2尺（≒1.27m）であった福山ほか（1975）：150ページ。
== 祭神 == [[祭神]]は、高水上命（たかみなかみのみこと）。内宮の[[神田]]を守護する[[水神]]である伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：58ページ。玉城町内にある内宮摂社の{{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社}}にも祀られている金子（1984）：59ページ。[[神体]]は[[石]]である。 元は[[天津神・国津神|国津神]]であったが、[[倭姫命]]に「田上神田」を差し出すことで神威を保った。 == 歴史 == 伝承によると、外城田川（寒川）を遡ってきた倭姫命を高水上命が出迎え、「田上神田」を献上したことから、坂手国生神社が祀られたという金子（1984）：54ページ。[[明治維新]]まで神社周辺に「田上神田」という内宮の御料田があり、毎年18俵の米を納めていた。 [[中世]]には廃絶し、[[寛文]]3年[[9月8日 (旧暦)|9月8日]]（[[グレゴリオ暦]]：[[1663年]][[10月8日]]）に{{ill|8=ja|9=棒原神社|Sugihara Shrine}}とともに[[河辺精長|河邊精長]]以下の[[神職]]らが[[遷宮]]を行って再興された式内社研究会 編（1990）：277ページ。再興まで社地が不明となっていたため、複数の候補地が挙げられていた。[[御巫清直]]は再興地が旧社地とは異なると非難している式内社研究会 編（1990）：278ページ。御巫は、古老からの聞き取りにより、上田辺の薬師堂の建つ丘が旧社地であるとした。 [[1904年]]（明治37年）4月に建て替えられた宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：11ページ。 == 祭祀 == 三節祭（[[月次祭]]・[[神嘗祭]]）、[[祈年祭]]、[[新嘗祭]]、臨時奉幣祭の際には神職らが社頭で祭祀を執り行う。 民間信仰では毎月1日と15日に参拝を行う者がある。[[太平洋戦争]]終結前までは近隣商店で鉄製の小型鳥居を買い求め、社頭に納める風習があった。また[[9月9日]]に「白石神事」が行われる{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=神宮ゆかりの地を巡る|publisher=玉城町役場産業振興課|work=のほほん たまき旅|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}。 == 交通 == [[伊勢参宮街道]]の北約300mの地点にある式内社研究会 編（1990）：279ページ。 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]] ** [[外城田駅]]より徒歩約26分（約2.1[[キロメートル|km]]）。 ** [[田丸駅]]より徒歩約47分（約2.5[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：57ページ。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[玉城インターチェンジ|玉城IC]]より約9分（約4.1[[キロメートル|km]]）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[田丸町]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise|さかてくなりしんしや}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[田丸町]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise|さかてくなりしんしや}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]

{{Shrine |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Tamaiki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Inner Shrine (Kōtai Jingū)|ja|皇大神宮}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It holds the 25th rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** - 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}}. - 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** - About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[田丸町]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise|さかてくなりしんしや}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]][[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]

{{Shrine |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Tamaiki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It holds the 25th rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** - 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}}. - 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** - About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It holds the 25th rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined Deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** - 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}}. - 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** - About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[田丸町]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise|さかてくなりしんしや}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Tamaiki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It holds the 25th rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** - 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}}. - 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** - About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[田丸町]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise|さかてくなりしんしや}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** - 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}}. - 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** - About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[田丸町]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise|さかてくなりしんしや}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命}}. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** - 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}}. - 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** - About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[田丸町]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise|さかてくなりしんしや}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:Sakatekunari Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "倭姫命}}" to "倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}"
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Tamaiki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It holds the 25th rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** - 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}}. - 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** - About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[田丸町]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise|さかてくなりしんしや}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:Sakatekunari Shrine]]

{{Shrine |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Tamaiki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It holds the 25th rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** - 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}}. - 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** - About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[田丸町]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise|さかてくなりしんしや}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:Sakatekunari Shrine]]

The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** - 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}}. - 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** - About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. {{Shrine |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Tamaiki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It holds the 25th rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** - 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}}. - 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** - About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[田丸町]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise|さかてくなりしんしや}} [[Category:Ugoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Categoery:qq]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Categoery:qq]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:Sakatekunari Shrine]]

ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} * [[田丸町]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise|さかてくなりしんしや}} [[Category:Ugoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category: :qq]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]][[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category: :qq]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sakatekunari Shrine-qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:Sakatekunari Shrine]]

{{Shrine |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Tamaiki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It holds the 25th rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** - 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅}}. - 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** - About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59年3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、平成2年2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p.
