revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tamaiki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It holds the 25th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]] [[sn:akatekunari Shrine]]

The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]] [[sn:akatekunari Shrine]]

{{Shrine |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tamaiki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It holds the 25th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:玉城町の建築物]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]] [[sn:akatekunari Shrine]]

The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:玉城町の歴史]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]] [[sn:akatekunari Shrine]]

{{Shrine |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tamaiki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It holds the 25th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]] [[sn:akatekunari Shrine]]

Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]] [[sn:akatekunari Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tamaiki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It holds the 25th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]] [[sn:akatekunari Shrine]]

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]] [[sn:akatekunari Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tamaiki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It holds the 25th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]] [[sn:akatekunari Shrine]]

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]] [[sn:akatekunari Shrine]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|国津神|" to "|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|"
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}}, {{ill|Tamaiki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaiki, Mie|qq=}}, Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation|qq=}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. It holds the 25th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|qq=}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina-kami|qq=}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/water deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin|qq=}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogosō Shrine|qq=}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shintai|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintai|qq=}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami (earthly deity)|qq=}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Ages of Japan|qq=}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Shrine|qq=}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Kiyonobu|qq=}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Sangu Kaidō|qq=}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoida Station|qq=}}. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Station|qq=}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]] [[sn:akatekunari Shrine]]

The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}}, the {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Sangu Kaidō|qq=}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoida Station|qq=}}. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Station|qq=}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]] [[sn:akatekunari Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}}, {{ill|Tamaiki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaiki, Mie|qq=}}, Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (the Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation|qq=}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} located in {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|ja|玉城町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki, Mie|qq=}}, {{ill|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai District|qq=}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}. It holds the 25th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|qq=}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the {{ill|shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} (sacred fence) and a {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}} gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina-kami|qq=}}. This deity is regarded as a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/water deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin|qq=}}, protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogosō Shrine|qq=}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The {{ill|shintai|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shintai|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintai|qq=}} (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami (earthly deity)|qq=}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}}. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the {{ill|Middle Ages of Japan|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Ages of Japan|qq=}}, but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Shrine|qq=}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Kiyonobu|qq=}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}}), the {{ill|Kinensai|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinensai|qq=}}, the {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}}, and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Sangu Kaidō|qq=}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from {{ill|Sotoida Station|ja|外城田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotoida Station|qq=}}. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from {{ill|Tamura Station|ja|田丸駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamura Station|qq=}}Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[sn:Sakatekunari Shrine]] [[sn:akatekunari Shrine]]
