{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17224770]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =File:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaiki, Mie]], Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie |location = {{ill|Tamaki|qid=Q587348}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.499111|N|136.617389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 用水路|1=irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2935978}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It holds the 25th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|shinmei-zukuri]] style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence) and a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This deity is regarded as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogosō Shrine|qq=}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami (earthly deity)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|6=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages of Japan]], but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Shrine|qq=}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Kiyonobu|qq=|1=Mikannagi Kiyonao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6537624}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]]), the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Sangu Kaidō|qq=|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]]. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]]Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q17224770}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17224770]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =File:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaiki, Mie]], Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie |location = {{ill|Tamaki|qid=Q587348}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.499111|N|136.617389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 用水路|1=irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2935978}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It holds the 25th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|shinmei-zukuri]] style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence) and a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This deity is regarded as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogosō Shrine|qq=}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami (earthly deity)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|6=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages of Japan]], but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Shrine|qq=}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Kiyonobu|qq=|1=Mikannagi Kiyonao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6537624}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]]), the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Sangu Kaidō|qq=|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]]. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]]Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q17224770}}

(1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Kiyonobu|qq=|1=Mikannagi Kiyonao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6537624}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]]), the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Sangu Kaidō|qq=|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]]. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]]Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] {{wikidata link|Q17224770}}

revision.text:
[[ja:坂手国生神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17224770]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =File:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaiki, Mie]], Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie |location = {{ill|Tamaki|qid=Q587348}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.499111|N|136.617389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 用水路|1=irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2935978}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It holds the 25th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|shinmei-zukuri]] style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence) and a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This deity is regarded as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogosō Shrine|qq=}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami (earthly deity)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|6=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages of Japan]], but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Shrine|qq=}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Kiyonobu|qq=|1=Mikannagi Kiyonao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6537624}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]]), the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Sangu Kaidō|qq=|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]]. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]]Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] {{wikidata link|Q17224770}}

In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Sangu Kaidō|qq=|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]]. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]]Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] {{wikidata link|Q17224770}}

revision.text:
[[ja:坂手国生神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17224770]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =File:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaiki, Mie]], Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie |location = {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.499111|N|136.617389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 用水路|1=irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2935978}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It holds the 25th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|shinmei-zukuri]] style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence) and a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This deity is regarded as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogosō Shrine|qq=}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami (earthly deity)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|6=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages of Japan]], but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Shrine|qq=}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Kiyonobu|qq=|1=Mikannagi Kiyonao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6537624}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]]), the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Sangu Kaidō|qq=|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]]. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]]Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] {{wikidata link|Q17224770}}

Until the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages of Japan]], but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Shrine|qq=}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Kiyonobu|1=Mikannagi Kiyonao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6537624}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]]), the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Sangu Kaidō|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]]. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]]Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] {{wikidata link|Q17224770}}

[[ja:坂手国生神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17224770]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =File:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaiki, Mie]], Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie |location = {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.499111|N|136.617389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 用水路|1=irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2935978}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It holds the 25th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|shinmei-zukuri]] style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence) and a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This deity is regarded as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogosō Shrine|qq=}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|6=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages of Japan]], but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Shrine|qq=}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Kiyonobu|1=Mikannagi Kiyonao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6537624}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]]), the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Sangu Kaidō|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]]. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]]Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] {{wikidata link|Q17224770}}

The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogosō Shrine|qq=}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|6=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages of Japan]], but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Shrine|qq=}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Kiyonobu|1=Mikannagi Kiyonao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6537624}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]]), the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Sangu Kaidō|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]]. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]]Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] {{wikidata link|Q17224770}}

revision.text:
[[ja:坂手国生神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17224770]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Sakatekunari Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image =File:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaiki, Mie]], Uedanabe, Ōyamada 2-144-1 |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 56.8 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 37 |Longitude Seconds = 2.6 |deity = Takamizukami no Mikoto |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} subordinate shrine |Established = Before Enryaku 23 (804 CE) |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Sakadaisan |Festivals = |Rituals = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] |Label Position = left |Map = Japan Mie |location = {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.499111|N|136.617389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Sakatekunari Shrine''' (坂手国生神社, *Sakatekunari-jinja*) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] that serves as a subordinate shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. The shrine's name is derived from its location atop a slope (*saka* meaning "slope"), and it enshrines a deity associated with {{ill|irrigation|ja|灌漑用水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/irrigation|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 用水路|1=irrigation canal|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2935978}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 67. == Sakatekunari Shrine == {{ill|Sakatekunari Shrine|ja|坂手国生神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakatekunari Shrine|qq=}} is one of the 13 auxiliary shrines (摂末社) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Tamaki, Mie|Tamaki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It holds the 25th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner ShrineUjiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–11. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as "Sakadaisan"Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 60Gakken Publishing (2013). == Overview == Located at 144-1 Ōyamada-ni, Kamitanui, Tamaki Town, Watarai District, Mie PrefectureTatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 48, the shrine sits atop a hill west of Ushiozaki Pond. The {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is accessible via a gentle stone stairway. The shrine grounds cover approximately 7,478 m², and the shrine is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|shinmei-zukuri]] style with a wooden shingle roofShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 280. It features a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence) and a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate. Historically, the shrine grounds spanned around 49,585 m². The ancient shrine building measured approximately 1.51 m in length, 1.09 m in width, and 1.27 m in heightFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. == Enshrined deity == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takamina-kami|ja|高水上命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamina-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This deity is regarded as a [[:en:Suijin|water god]], protecting the sacred rice fields (神田) of the Inner ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p. 58. The same deity is also worshipped at {{ill|Ogosō Shrine|ja|小社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogosō Shrine|qq=}} in Tamaki TownKaneko (1984): p. 59. The [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] (sacred object) is a stone. Originally classified as a {{ill|kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kunitsukami (earthly deity)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|6=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}, Takamina-kami maintained its divine status by offering "Tagami Kanda" to [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. == History == According to legend, when Yamato-hime-no-mikoto ascended the Sotoda River (Kanagawa), she was greeted by Takamina-kami, who presented her with "Tagami Kanda," leading to the establishment of Sakatekunari ShrineKaneko (1984): p. 54. Until the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], there were sacred rice fields known as "Tagami Kanda" around the shrine, which annually contributed 18 koku of rice to the Inner Shrine. The shrine fell into disuse during the [[:en:Middle Ages|Middle Ages of Japan]], but was restored on October 8, 1663, alongside {{ill|Sugihara Shrine|ja|棒原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugihara Shrine|qq=}}, under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} and other Shinto priestsShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 277. Since the original shrine site had been lost, several potential sites were proposed. {{ill|Mikami Kiyonobu|ja|御巫清直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikami Kiyonobu|1=Mikannagi Kiyonao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6537624}} criticized the restored location, claiming it differed from the originalShikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 278. According to oral traditions, the old shrine site was where Yakushidō now stands on a nearby hill. The current shrine building was reconstructed in April 1904Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 11. == Rituals == Shinto priests perform rituals during the three main festivals ({{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}}, [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]]), the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]], the [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]], and temporary ritual offerings (臨時奉幣祭). In folk belief, worshippers often visit the shrine on the 1st and 15th of each month. Until the end of World War II, it was customary to purchase small iron torii gates from nearby shops and dedicate them to the shrine. On September 9, the "White Stone Ritual" (白石神事) is held{{Cite web|url=http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|title=巡る神宮ゆかりの地|publisher=Tamaki Town Tourism Division|accessdate=2014-06-15|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140601033540/http://www.tamaki-kanko.jp/html/special_jingu-yukari.html|archivedate=2014-06-01}}. == Access == The shrine is located about 300 m north of the {{ill|Ise Sangu Kaidō|ja|伊勢参宮街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Sangu Kaidō|1=Ise Sangū Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378967}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 279. * **By JR Sangu Line:** ** 26-minute walk (approx. 2.1 km) from [[:en:Tokida Station|Sotoida Station]]. ** 47-minute walk (approx. 2.5 km) from [[:en:Tamaru Station|Tamura Station]]Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 57. * **By Car:** ** About 9 minutes (approx. 4.1 km) from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第3巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第92集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和59th year 3月10日、308p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[田丸町]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_07.jpg 坂手国生神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m08/0801.html 坂手国生神社（さかてくなりじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 * [http://www.town.tamaki.mie.jp/tamnet/sankan/jinjya/ 産業観光神社めぐり] - 三重県玉城町 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|坂手国生神社|version=97953568|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[file:Sakatekunari-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] [[ja:坂手国生神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] {{wikidata link|Q17224770}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
